sections.paragraph: and עֻגָּה-( 1Ki 19:6 Eze 4:12), fem. a cake baked under hot cinders, such as the Orientals are still accustomed to make, especially when on a journey, or in haste; see Rosenm. Morgenland, i. p. 69; עֻגַּת רְצָפִים a cake baked on hot stones, 1Ki 19:6 עֻגֹּות מַצֹות unleavened cakes, Exo 12:39. (Arab. عُجَّةُ a cake made with eggs, baked in a pan). Root עוּג which see.
